text.title: AI-Assisted Specification Writing (Planning Layer) Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the ai-assisted-specification-writing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Plan MUST NOT be drafted while spec.md has `status != approved` or open AC.
rule.rationale: Drafting against unstable spec produces a plan that needs full rework after AC change.

rule.statement: Task graph MUST be acyclic — circular dependencies are rejected.
rule.rationale: Cycles deadlock execution and indicate hidden coupling.

rule.statement: Every task MUST carry `est_tokens` (integer); missing values blocked.
rule.rationale: Without est_tokens capacity planning is impossible.

rule.statement: Critical-path tasks MUST be flagged `cp: true` in the plan.
rule.rationale: Without CP flag teams parallelize the wrong work.

rule.statement: Human reviewer MUST sign off the dependency graph; LLM-only graphs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: LLM-drafted graphs commonly invert subtle dependencies.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
